{"key_event":"WTO boom and adaptive Party-state expansion","reasoning":"The WTO-era boom, fiscal growth, surveillance/security buildout, Olympics, stimulus, and stability-maintenance apparatus pushed an already strong Party-state into still deeper administrative and coercive penetration. Civil society gained real but constrained capacity through the internet, NGOs, rights lawyers, private property claims, protests, and volunteering, yet co-optation, censorship, and crackdowns kept it in the weak range.","society_change":0.8,"society_power":4.3,"state_change":0.5,"state_power":9.2}
